steps.title: Eliminate symmetric distributions
steps.content:
Mean equals median in symmetric distributions.
  • Choice A: constant 5s — symmetric
  • Choice B: 0, 10, 20, …, 80 (arithmetic sequence) — symmetric
Both have mean = median. For mean > median, we need right-skewed data where large values pull the mean upward.

steps.title: Identify exponential growth in choice C
steps.content:
Choice C: 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512

This doubles each term, creating severe right skew. The massive values at the end (256, 512) pull the mean far above the median.
  • Median = 5th value = 32
  • Sum = 1022
  • Mean = 1022 ÷ 9 ≈ 113.6
Since 113.6 > 32, the mean exceeds the median. That matches choice C — C is correct.
